title: EpiMII: Structure-Aware Graph Neural Networks for MHC-II Epitope Generation
excerpt: EpiMII is a computational tool utilizing structure-aware graph neural networks to predict and generate major histocompatibility complex class II (MHC-II) epitopes. The approach integrates structural information through graph-based machine learning to improve epitope identification, supporting immunoinformatics applications in vaccine and therapeutic development.

title: Advances in microneedle design for the delivery of drugs, proteins, and cells in the treatment of hypertrophic scars
excerpt: This review examines advances in microneedle design for transdermal and intradermal delivery of drugs, proteins, and cells as therapeutic approaches for hypertrophic scar treatment. Microneedle technology offers minimally invasive delivery mechanisms that can enhance drug penetration and localized therapeutic effects in scar tissue. The article discusses innovations in microneedle formulation and design strategies relevant to burn trauma and scar management applications.

title: A Medicinal Chemistry Perspective on Anticonvulsant Drug Design: Targets, Mechanisms, and Spiro Compounds
excerpt: This review examines anticonvulsant drug design from a medicinal chemistry perspective, focusing on established drug targets, their mechanisms of action, and the role of spiro compounds as a structural class in anticonvulsant development. The analysis integrates current understanding of molecular targets with chemical design strategies to advance anticonvulsant therapeutics.

title: The Systematic Study of Spatially Conserved Salt Bridges in Protein
excerpt: This systematic study examines spatially conserved salt bridges in proteins, analyzing their structural characteristics and potential significance. title: Long-Term Glycemic Control and Pharmacotherapy in Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus: A Descriptive Analysis of 10-Year Trends in a Statewide Sample
excerpt: This descriptive analysis examines 10-year trends in glycemic control outcomes and medication use patterns for type 2 diabetes patients across a statewide population sample. The study tracks long-term pharmacotherapy approaches and their association with sustained blood glucose management over the decade-long observation period. The findings provide insights into evolving treatment strategies and glycemic control achievement in type 2 diabetes management at the population level.
